page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 virtual CPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a 2850 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high-throughput networking. The instance achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 15879.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 48761.09, both placing in the strong tier. Cryptographic performance is also notable, with a Passmark encryption test score of 43656.33. Static web serving throughput reached 40.53 GB/sec, while image processing benchmarks also demonstrated strong performance. Other metrics, such as single-core stress-ng and LLM inference speeds, align with average mid-tier expectations for this hardware class.

From a resource density perspective, the m6idn.12xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency by combining high-speed local NVMe storage, dedicated compute resources, and high-bandwidth networking on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. This configuration avoids the need for separate network-attached storage for high-I/O operations, optimizing resource utilization. The server is well-suited for workloads requiring a balance of compute and fast local storage, such as database operations, web serving, containerized applications, and distributed file systems.
description: The Amazon Web Services m6idn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance powered by a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 48 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance features a bundled 2850 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and a high-speed 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 48761.09 and a static web serving throughput of 40.53 GB/sec. It is designed for workloads requiring balanced compute, high-speed local storage, and high network throughput, such as database hosting, web serving, and data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.12xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, a 2850 GB NVMe SSD, and 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput database and web workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose platform combining dedicated Intel Xeon compute, high-speed local NVMe storage, and 75 Gbps baseline networking.

192.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Bundled 2850 GB NVMe SSD local instance storage
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput applications
